mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits an easing discount-rate shock to equities, raising S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days because lower short rates reduce required returns and support valuation multiples.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is falling for at least 2 consecutive months, or if the rolling 2-year hit rate drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in UNRATE signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings downside, which should pull S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days once unemployment jumps by more than 0.3 percentage points from the prior month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST and RSAFS both accelerate together, the mechanism indicates broad demand stabilization through housing-linked spending and retail turnover, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising HOUST and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60% of joint-acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO above its recent trend signals stronger industrial output and better corporate revenue prospects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if breakouts above the 3-month moving average are followed by negative or flat 20-day S&P 500 returns more often than positive returns over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS together with a rising UNRATE indicates simultaneous payroll contraction and unemployment stress, which should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings expectations are repriced downward quickly.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-loss and unemployment-rise condition does not produce negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if its information ratio is not above zero over a full expansion-contraction cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable CPIAUCSL combined with a near-zero FEDFUNDS rate signals an accommodative liquidity regime that sustains equity multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-inflation, near-zero-rate episodes fail to outperform the unconditional 60-day return distribution, or if positive returns occur in less than 55% of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A steep decline in RSAFS after a prior expansion signals immediate consumer-demand softening and revenue disappointment, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend retail-sales episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the average forward return is not at least 0.5 percentage points below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing rebound regime, rising INDPRO paired with rising PAYEMS confirms a broad production and hiring upswing that should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through stronger cyclical earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in industrial production and payrolls does not outperform either component alone, or if the triggered 20-day hit rate falls below 57% over rolling 3-year windows.
